remember: Students will be able to recall the poem 'A Thing of Beauty' by John Keats.
Students will be able to identify the speaker and his emotions in the poem.
Students will be able to list the key features of the poem.
understand: Students will be able to interpret the meaning of the poem's title 'A Thing of Beauty'.
Students will be able to explain the significance of the odes in the Romantic period.
Students will be able to identify the use of imagery and symbolism in the poem.
apply: Students will be able to apply the poem's themes to everyday life.
Students will be able to use the poem's language to describe a beautiful scene.
Students will be able to analyze the speaker's tone and attitude in the poem.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the structure and poetic devices used in the poem.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the poem with other odes from the Romantic period.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of the poem's themes on the reader.

ncert_objectives: To understand the poem 'A Thing of Beauty' as a representative of the Romantic era.
To analyze the themes, imagery, and symbolism used in the poem.
To evaluate the significance of the poem in the context of Romantic poetry.
board_exam_objectives: To identify and explain the key features of the poem 'A Thing of Beauty'.
To analyze the language, structure, and poetic devices used in the poem.
To apply the poem's themes to everyday life and evaluate its impact on the reader.
